message:
[MED] fcp-protocol: cap missing_hint before signature transcript (DoS)

DecodeStatus::verify() and SymbolRequest::verify() both called
transcript_bytes() — which iterates the full missing_hint vector
into the transcript — *before* the Ed25519 check. An attacker could
send a message with a forged signature and a missing_hint holding
millions of entries: every receiver would allocate ~4 bytes/entry
and hash the result before rejecting, giving unauthenticated senders
a cheap amplification lever against whole zones.

Route both verify() paths through validate_hint_bounds() first and
collapse an over-cap hint into SignatureVerificationFailed (already
the NORMATIVE rule per the DecodeStatus anti-amplification section),
so malformed oversized messages are rejected before touching the
transcript builder. Add two regression tests that sign an over-cap
hint and assert verify() returns SignatureVerificationFailed despite
the signature being otherwise valid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
